_README: Copy this file, rename it, and edit the values for your project. Keys starting with '_' are ignored (use them for notes). Figure 'path' values are resolved against the base_dir you pass to the builder. Text supports bold and italic.
title: My Project Report
subtitle: One-line description of the project
intro: One short paragraph stating the goal of the project and what this report covers.

blocks.text: Explain in plain English what the project does and why it matters.
Describe the dataset(s): source, size, features, target.
2. Problems Encountered & How They Were Solved

blocks._note: Use style: info (blue), warning (orange), danger (red), success (green).
blocks.title: Problem 1 — short title
blocks.text: Describe the problem, then Solution: describe the fix.

blocks.title: Problem 2 — short title
blocks.text: Describe the problem, then Solution: describe the fix.

items: Explore: what you found in the data.
Train & tune: models tried and how they were validated.

blocks._note: Replace path with your figure; width_cm controls size.
blocks.caption: Caption explaining what this chart shows.

blocks.text: Summarise how models performed on held-out data.

items: Accuracy — share of all cases classified correctly.
Precision — when it predicts positive, how often it is right (few false alarms).
Recall — of all real positives, how many were caught (few misses).
F1 — single balanced score combining precision and recall.
ROC AUC — overall ability to separate the classes; 1.0 is perfect.

blocks.text: Which features drive the predictions, and why that makes sense.

blocks.title: Limitations to keep in mind
blocks.text: State anything that could make real-world performance differ from the reported scores.
